member.name: T:System.Collections.Generic.IFiniteSet`2
member.summary: Represents an object that is a set of elements of type , and is a closed set, meaning the set contains concept "all", i.e. set can be negated.

self-type-constraint.para: Indicates the type implementing this interface.

This type doesn't use any extra syntax to constraint such usage, but C# will check for it. The type will include a constraint that implements the interface itself to declare such "self constraint" usage: interface ISelfType<TSelf> where TSelf : ISelfType<TSelf> In addition, the type should be named TSelf in order to tell type parameters with the others.

member.name: M:System.Collections.Generic.IFiniteSet`2.Negate
member.summary: Returns an object that inverts elements including the current collection.

member.name: T:System.Collections.Generic.IInfiniteSet`2
member.summary: Represents an object that is a set of elements of type , and isn't a closed set, meaning the set doesn't contain concept "all", i.e. set cannot be negated.

self-type-constraint.para: Indicates the type implementing this interface.

This type doesn't use any extra syntax to constraint such usage, but C# will check for it. The type will include a constraint that implements the interface itself to declare such "self constraint" usage: interface ISelfType<TSelf> where TSelf : ISelfType<TSelf> In addition, the type should be named TSelf in order to tell type parameters with the others.

member.name: T:System.ILogicalOperators`1
member.summary: Defines a mechanism for computing the logical relation between two instances of type .

self-type-constraint.para: Indicates the type implementing this interface.

This type doesn't use any extra syntax to constraint such usage, but C# will check for it. The type will include a constraint that implements the interface itself to declare such "self constraint" usage: interface ISelfType<TSelf> where TSelf : ISelfType<TSelf> In addition, the type should be named TSelf in order to tell type parameters with the others.

self-type-constraint.para: Indicates the type implementing this interface.

This type doesn't use any extra syntax to constraint such usage, but C# will check for it. The type will include a constraint that implements the interface itself to declare such "self constraint" usage: interface ISelfType<TSelf> where TSelf : ISelfType<TSelf> In addition, the type should be named TSelf in order to tell type parameters with the others.

target.name: generic-method
target.para: Starting with C# 14, the extension member feature is supported. When an block declaration has generic parameters that are used by its formal parameters, the method members inside the block will prepend those generic parameters so that they become part of the method's signature.

For example, if you have extension<T>(T instance) that contains a method DoSomething<TAnother>(), then the actual generic parameters of DoSomething are DoSomething<T, TAnother>. Even if T does not need to be specified, when TAnother must be provided, both generic parameters still need to be declared, following the rules of generic methods in C#.

target.name: generic-method
target.para: Starting with C# 14, the extension member feature is supported. When an block declaration has generic parameters that are used by its formal parameters, the method members inside the block will prepend those generic parameters so that they become part of the method's signature.

For example, if you have extension<T>(T instance) that contains a method DoSomething<TAnother>(), then the actual generic parameters of DoSomething are DoSomething<T, TAnother>. Even if T does not need to be specified, when TAnother must be provided, both generic parameters still need to be declared, following the rules of generic methods in C#.

member.name: T:System.Linq.Providers.ILinqMethod`2
member.summary: Represents a type that supports a certain method group defined in , well-known as LINQ methods.

self-type-constraint.para: Indicates the type implementing this interface.

This type doesn't use any extra syntax to constraint such usage, but C# will check for it. The type will include a constraint that implements the interface itself to declare such "self constraint" usage: interface ISelfType<TSelf> where TSelf : ISelfType<TSelf> In addition, the type should be named TSelf in order to tell type parameters with the others.

member.typeparam: The type of each element that the type supports for iteration.

member.name: P:System.Linq.Providers.ILinqMethod`2.IsExtensionMethod
member.summary: Indicates whether the method group supported is extension method.

member.name: P:System.Linq.Providers.ILinqMethod`2.IsValueLazilyCalculated
member.summary: Indicates whether the provider type won't calculate the final result immediately. The value can be if such calculation is applied like values. By default, the value is .

member.name: P:System.Linq.Providers.ILinqMethod`2.SupportsQuerySyntax
member.summary: Indicates whether the methods defined in type support for query syntax also. By default, the value is .
member.remarks:
All possible query clauses are:
Query syntax Equivalent method
element collection N/A
element1 collection1 element2 collection2
condition
variable = expression
{ variable | expression } [ variable]
{ variable | expression } ?
{ variable | expression }
{ v1 | e1 }, { v2 | e2 } ? ...
{ v1 | e1 }, { v2 | e2 } ...
variable { variable | expression } [ variable]
expression expression [ variable]
variable collection previousVariable variable
variable2 collection2 variable1 variable2 joinedGroup
variable2 collection2 variable1 variable2
LeftJoin (will be added in future version of .NET library)
variable2 collection2 variable1 variable2
RightJoin (will be added in future version of .NET library)

feature.name: scoped-keyword
feature.para: The keyword means the argument and local variable can only be used inside the method. This means you cannot use this variable and argument outside the scope, such as new RefStructType(scoped_variable), return scoped_variable and field = scoped_variable. Such expressions may store the variables, unsafely expanding the variable's lifecycle.

summary.para: Provides with the type that contains a module initializer method, called automatically by the CLR (Common Language Runtime) to initialize some values.

target.name: method
target.summary: Called by the runtime automatically while booting on this solution to initialize the values, data members or any other things that can or should be initialized here.
target.remarks: The concept module is different with assembly. The solution can contain multiple assemblies, while each assembly can contain multiple modules. However, due to the design of Visual Studio project file system, each assembly will only contain one module by default.

member.name: M:System.Resources.SR.RegisterResourceManager``1
member.summary: Register a new resource manager for the current assembly calling this method.

typeparam.name: TResourceManagerProvider
typeparam.para: The type of the resource manager provider.

This type should point to a generated type, bound with your resource dictionary manifest file (*.resx), named like the file name of it. For example, if you create a resource dictionary manifest file called Resource.resx, a generated type will be named Resource. You should pass in this type (Resource here) as type argument to this method.

member.exception: Throws when the current calling assembly doesn't contain any resource manager.

The maximum number of elements in each chunk. Must be greater than zero.
